steps: Use the rule \(\sqrt{x} \times \sqrt{y} = \sqrt{xy}\) to combine the two radicals: \(\sqrt{(a+c)^3} \times \sqrt{a+c} = \sqrt{(a+c)^3 \cdot (a+c)} = \sqrt{(a+c)^4}\).

• Choice A — '\(a+c\)': This would require the expression to equal \((a+c)^1\), but we derived \((a+c)^2\). ✗
  • Choice B — '\(a^2+c^2\)': Missing the cross term \(2ac\); this is \((a+c)^2\) only if \(ac=0\), which is impossible since both are positive. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(a^2+2ac+c^2\)': Exactly the expansion of \((a+c)^2\). ✓
  • Choice D — '\(a^2 \times c^2\)': This equals \((ac)^2\), a product, which has no relation to \((a+c)^2\). ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
